title: Pan-Seared Lamb Kebabs with Ethiopian Spices
description: A flavorful and aromatic dish that combines the taste of lamb with the warm spices of Ethiopia. Perfect for a quick dinner or gathering.

ingredients.name: Lamb shoulder, boneless and cut into 1-inch cubes
ingredients.notes: Make sure to trim any excess fat

ingredients.name: Ethiopian spice mix
ingredients.notes: You can also use store-bought Ethiopian spice mix

ingredients.name: Tangy yogurt
ingredients.notes: Use plain or flavored yogurt, depending on your preference

ingredients.name: Pillowy naan bread or flatbread
ingredients.notes: You can also serve with rice or injera

ingredients.name: Curry powder
ingredients.notes: Add this for extra warmth and depth

ingredients.name: Olive oil
ingredients.notes: For brushing the lamb and naan

instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together the Ethiopian spice mix, yogurt, and curry powder. Add the lamb and mix until well coated.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes or up to 2 hours.
Thread the lamb cubes onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Brush the lamb with olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
Heat a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat. Add the lamb skewers and cook for 2-3 minutes per side, or until browned.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and cook for 10-12 minutes, or until the lamb reaches an internal temperature of 145°F (63°C) for medium-rare.
Remove the skillet from the oven and let the lamb rest for 5 minutes.
Serve the lamb with naan bread or flatbread, and enjoy!
Note: Make sure to cook the lamb to the recommended internal temperature to ensure food safety.
